LaurierUBBusiness Administration (BBA)Offer89.211/7/202511/25/2025Group ACanadianOntarioAIF
Two exchange years, business owner generating 4 figures of income each month, 500+ work hours, leader at best buddies club
I had a 68% in grade 11 english, and some other poor grades too so I made sure to dial in for the grade 12 versions. On the AIF I talked about my experience at the best buddies club and navigating my emotions while working with people affected by disabilities. I have some pretty notable EC’s, but as long as you’re brief and honest you should be ok.

LaurierUZArts (Communication Studies)Offer959/30/202511/3/2025Group B
Russian Federation
Korea
Applicant Background Summary
Varsity Cross Country Team (2 years); Varsity Track and Field Team (1 year); Participated and lettered through running 5000m, 3200m, 1600m, and all throwing events; Swim Manager (3 years); Managed the student-athletes in the varsity swim team. I tracked their attendance, necessary forms, and times; Cross Country Manager (2 years); Managed the student-athletes in the varsity cross country team by tracking their attendance, times, going to meets to get unofficial times, etc.; Red Cross Youth (2 years); Volunteered by spending time on posters, promoting blood drives locally, and fundraising for the Red Cross Korea organization; Hitch Hiker’s Guide (4 years); Helped with bake sales for fundraising and participated in the artwork submissions; Film Club (1 year); Helped create and film promotional videos for theatre productions and the school’s winter banquet; Ossembly Student Leaders (1.5 years); Part of a group of students responsible for creating presentations, lesson plans, and activities for the high school OASIS (non-Christian assembly) students every week; Theatre Hair and Make-up Crew; Theatre Hair and Make-up Crew Manager' Managing the different make-up looks for the school’s theatre productions, and organizing materials; ISS Rotary Club (2 years); Helped with volunteering at several shelters throughout 2023-2025; ConnectArt (2 years); A member of the student-led club for connecting children with different forms of art, such as creating music and paintings. Helped volunteer for booths to fundraise for the club twice a year; Children’s Face Painting Volunteer (4 years); Helped face-paint children for 3 hours at school fundraising booths twice a year since 2022; High school-wide art exhibition committee co-director (1 year); Co-directing student-led high school art exhibitions, creating a committee, planning dates, and organizing communication with the school; Prom Committee (1 year); Helped organize the Junior-Senior prom in 2025, creating the theme and planning out the decor. Helping envision the posters and create promotional materials.
AP courses for Group B applicants barely matter, unless you want them for your 4UM courses, 4+ score can be used as credit.

UofT - SGTADComputer ScienceOffer97.6712/2/20251/27/2026Group ACanadianOntario
yes, 4 short paragraph questions
Made a hackathon with a good amount of attendees (100+), student government leaderships (at school and in board), math competition prep, I focused on really showing how these activities were done out of interest and how they connect to problem solving perspectives, i also talked about certain courses at uoft in the mathematical area of interest I have and my desire to take them.

1868
UofT - SGTCSComputer EngineeringOffer9712/2/20252/13/2026Group ACanadianOntario
Interview, Extracurricular Section
Pretty good ECs, decent interview. Put a lot of effort into revising the 250 word question with other people, and refining my talking skills in front of a camera before taking the interview.
In my opinion, you need at least a week of effort for this application, and you should work at it every day. Draft that 250 words and take it seriously by consulting other people and taking their feedback. I put 3 jot notes per extracurricular in the boxes. Prepare for the interview by getting comfortable with speaking in front of a camera with their sample questions in the practice module. Good luck, YOU GOT THIS!!

Ivey Essays and KIRA interview
Wrote a 25-page Economics Research paper through a program I got into with full-ride scholarship. Self-studied 3 AP courses. National-level executive of a business club, UofT Math Enrichment program (x2), 300hrs of volunteering. Included many awards, including a medal in an international Economics competition, Waterloo Math contest distinctions, and sports achievements.
Feel free to reach out to me on Discord: hobidik99. Happy to discuss my application further or read the essays/applications of applicants for next cycles.

WesternAEOIvey AEOOffer94.81/15/20263/6/2026Group ACanadianOntario
KIRA, 2 essays, awards, additional activities
2 essays were about starting and coaching a youth sports team (a sport i grew up in), and being deca president and using my experience i got from going to ICDC to transform our chapter and help others succeed. my additional activities were student council president, mock trial exec member, sports coach (a job), and my sport.
make sure you follow the CAR method (context, action, result) focus on IMPACT. how it impacted you, others, and your community. show meaningful change, that you made a real difference. quantify things as much as you can to show impact.
